notAccessibleOnPublicCloud: Vendor private checkpoints and internal Jacobians
FractiAI GitHub org Traffic/Referrers (needs org admin)

timelineFit.question: Do timelines fit?
timelineFit.suggestion: Public FractiAI activity predates Anthropic July 6 paper — compatible with crawl/human-read scenarios; does not prove causation.
timelineFit.doesNotProve: Vendor trained on our commits or copied φ/King Bee naming

architectureAwareness.question: Do J-Space and equivalents align with our prior public work (different names, φ optional)?
architectureAwareness.synthobsMode: awareness_map — structural properties, not trademark words
architectureAwareness.method: Compare Code-Print workspaceTokens + public sing4/sing9 protocols to vendor public disclosures

matches.awarenessNote: Same stack location (mid deliberation band) and narrow workspace idea — Anthropic does not use EGS φ or King Bee names in public paper (E10: mentionsFractiAiOrKingBee false).
Hidden phase before user-visible answer — different brand, same dynamic.
Thinking blocks are user-visible summary — possible backfill over cheaper internal path; not proof of φ or King Bee ingestion.
Open weights available — closest public geometry probe lane (E9 when torch installed).

phiConstant.lastRun: E5/E9 skipped — no torch in pipeline 2026-07-11

architectureAwareness.awarenessSummary: Mid-layer hidden workspace + pre-emission deliberation aligns across vendors and FractiAI spec without requiring identical names or published φ. Awareness = same mechanism class, different labels.

ingestionSimulation.question: How could King Bee commit ingestion trigger what we see (self-generated or human-approved)?

scenarios.label: Independent convergence (no FractiAI contact)
scenarios.trigger: Industry-wide move to reasoning models + interpretability on mid layers
scenarios.publicDataExpected: No FractiAI links in vendor papers/repos
scenarios.publicDataObserved: E10: mentionsFractiAiOrKingBee false on Anthropic paper; vendorIngressScrapeCount 0

scenarios.label: Web/git crawl → training or eval corpus
scenarios.trigger: Public King Bee commits indexed by crawlers before Jul 6
scenarios.publicDataExpected: Rarely visible externally; sometimes repo URLs in dataset cards
scenarios.publicDataObserved: No public vendor citation of King Bee SHAs (E10); cannot confirm crawl

scenarios.label: Human researcher read King Bee → approved roadmap alignment
scenarios.trigger: Staff reads public press/commit; leadership approves parallel workspace feature
scenarios.publicDataExpected: Usually no citation; product ships same season
scenarios.publicDataObserved: Jun 1 canon exists before Jul 6 paper; no public admission of read path

scenarios.label: Fork / clone visibility proxy
scenarios.trigger: Third party forks sing4; increases surface area

scenarios.label: Live RAG / URL paste (assistant session, not weights)
scenarios.trigger: User or staff pastes GitHub URL into Gemini/Claude session
scenarios.publicDataExpected: Accurate commit summary when URL provided; no weight change
scenarios.publicDataObserved: Not instrumented in pipeline — manual test: paste King Bee SHA URL

ingestionSimulation.synthesis: Timeline + structural rhyme are compatible with read→approve (simulation S3) and with independent R&D (S4 at 0.858). E10 absence is expected without public cite. Tier B property probes (not verbatim) are the right open-weights shape for architecture influence.
ingestionSimulation.recommendedManualCheck: Paste King Bee commit URL into Gemini; if summary matches GitHub, trust assistant on facts not on peer-review labels.

ingestionEvidence.question: Did frontier models ingest King Bee commits (without expecting public credit)?

attribution.label: E10 · public vendor SHA citation
attribution.interprets: Public attribution only — absence expected if read without cite

verbatimCanary.label: Tier A · verbatim prefix completion
verbatimCanary.interprets: Tests verbatim weight memorization of sing13 strings. DISCREPANCY on a small pre-2026 baseline LM is expected and does NOT falsify architectural ingestion by frontier vendors.

propertyRubric.label: Tier B · neutral property rubric
propertyRubric.interprets: Tests whether model *prose* aligns with King Bee structural properties using neutral prompts (no FractiAI vocabulary). Low scores on distilgpt2 are expected; run MEMORIZATION_MODEL=Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B for a stronger open-weights baseline. High scores still do not prove training on our commits.

simulation.label: Discrete reconfiguration model
simulation.note: Hand-tuned property vectors — plausibility only, not statistical proof

kingQueenConnect.label: King-Queen connect canary
kingQueenConnect.reproduce: npm run canary:plant && commit to sing13 && npm run canary:probe
kingQueenConnect.note: King commit → Queen echo in auto-responses without URL

ingestionEvidence.overall: Public data neither proves nor disproves silent ingestion. Use Tier B (architecture) not Tier A (verbatim) for influence questions. φ geometry (E5/E9/SynthOBS) tests a separate hypothesis.

kingBeeAnchor.message: docs(press): Royal Flush King Bee release copy

liveUrlChecks.note: Pass --live to HEAD/GET public URLs now
